message:
fix(rpc,sse,providers): backpressure handling, parser allocation reduction, TextEnd event

Three targeted fixes across the streaming infrastructure:

1. RPC stdin backpressure (rpc.rs): Extract try_send_line_with_backpressure()
   to properly detect receiver disconnection (channel closed or cancelled)
   and break the stdin reader loop instead of spinning forever. The previous
   inline retry loop would spin indefinitely when the agent exited, keeping
   the stdin reader thread alive. New function returns false on disconnect,
   allowing clean thread exit. Includes unit tests for both success and
   disconnect paths.

2. SSE parser allocation reduction (sse.rs): Refactor the SSE parser to use
   a feed_into() callback pattern that emits events directly to the consumer
   (typically a VecDeque) without constructing an intermediate Vec. The
   public feed() method still returns Vec<SseEvent> for backward compatibility
   by collecting from feed_into(). SseStream internally uses feed_to_pending()
   to push events directly into pending_events, eliminating Vec allocation
   and extend() overhead on every chunk.

3. OpenAI Responses TextEnd event (providers/openai_responses.rs): Add
   StreamEvent::TextEnd variant handling to the test EventSummary mapper,
   capturing content_index and accumulated content. Prevents test panics
   when stream fixtures include text_end events.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1393,6 +1393,17 @@ mod tests {
                 content: None,
                 reason: None,
             },
+            StreamEvent::TextEnd {
+                content_index,
+                content,
+                ..
+            } => EventSummary {
+                kind: "text_end".to_string(),
+                content_index: Some(*content_index),
+                delta: None,
+                content: Some(content.clone()),
+                reason: None,
+            },
             StreamEvent::Done { reason, .. } => EventSummary {
                 kind: "done".to_string(),
                 content_index: None,
